original: Meteor falls near Estherville, Iowa
Lee Chin Yong performs 170 continuous chin-ups in Seoul
Ellen Ochoa, PhD/Astronaut (STS 56, 66), born in Los Angeles, California
25°F lowest temperature ever recorded in Cleveland in May
French TGV-train hits record speed of 510.6 kph (317.3 mph)
China announces plans for the world's largest weather-control mechanism, rain-inducing machines for the Tibetan Plateau (area the size of Alaska)
Stanley Cup Final, Boston Garden, Boston, MA:Bobby Orrscores a famous OT winner as Boston beats the St. Louis Blues 4-3 for a 4-0 series sweep; Bruins win their first title since 1941
Nelson Mandelais sworn in as South Africa's first Black president
US atomic submarine USS Triton completes first submerged circumnavigation of the globe
American Revolution: Green Mountain Boys capture Fort Ticonderoga, New York
Halley's Comet makes its closest approach to Earth in 1910 pass
First US planetarium opens, the Adler Planetarium in Chicago
British Longitude Act awards clockmakerJohn Harrison£10,000 for the invention of a naval longitude clock
The Golden Spike is driven, completing the first US Transcontinental Railroad at Promontory Summit, Utah, connecting theCentral Pacific Railroadwith the Union Pacific
Augustin-Jean Fresnel, French physicist and optics pioneer (proved wave theory of light correct, invented catadioptric Fresnel lens), born in Broglie, France (d. 1827)
Michael Larson, American "Press Your Luck" scandal big-winning contestant, born in Lebanon, Ohio (d. 1999)
Yanks commit 8 errors & still beat Tigers 10-9 in 10 innings
Golf ball size hail falls in Burlington, New Jersey
Red Sox win 15th straight beat Yanks 5-4, DiMaggio hits Grand Slam
Bolshoi Ballet does not appear in Paris
Z Alexander Looby elected to Nashville City Council
Tornado outbreak sequence takes place across the Great Plains and Eastern United States
Kara Walker's artwork "The Subtlety" opens - an installation of a sphinx made of sugar at the Domino Sugar factory in Brooklyn, NY
Oliver Selfridge, English-American computer scientist and leading artificial intelligence researcher known as the "father of machine perception", born in London (d. 2008)
George E. Smith, American physicist and co-inventor of the charge-coupled device who was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics (2009), born in White Plain, New York (d. 2025)[1]
James E. Chaney, American civil rights activist who helped organize the Freedom Summer campaign, born in Meridian, Mississippi (d. 1964)
Judith Jamison, American dancer and choreographer (artistic director of the Alvin Ailey Dance Theater), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 2024)[1]
Jim Abrahams, American comedy screenwriter and director (Airplane!; Hot Shots; Top Secret; The Naked Gun), born in Shorewood, Wisconsin (d. 2024)[1][2]
Essex County, Massachusetts is founded, which contains the town of Salem in which the notorious witch-trials will occur in 1692
Jem Mace defends his heavyweight crown against Irish champ Joe Coburn; the match lasts 1 hour and 17 minutes, and neither is struck by a punch
Challenger transports back to Kennedy Space Center via Kelly AFB
DGC Records releases "Weezer" (known as The Blue Album), the debut album of rock band Weezer
India's population reaches 1 billion, baby girl Aastha born at Delhi's Safdarjung Hospital chosen as symbolic billionth
Dmitri Shostakovich's 2nd Piano concerto premieres, played by his son Maxim at his graduation concert at Moscow's conservatory
Alan Bennett, Peter Cook,Dudley Mooreand Jonathan Miller comedy revue "Beyond the Fringe" opens at the Fortune Theatre in London's West End
Decca Records signsThe Rolling Stoneson the advice of Beatles guitaristGeorge Harrison
USGS releases a report saying that some glaciers in Montana have receded by 85% in the last 50 years
George Welch, American World War II flying ace (Medal of Honor nominee) and test pilot, born in Wawaset Park, Delaware (d. 1954)

Arthur Alexander, American singer-songwriter ("Lonely Just Like Me"), whose songs were covered by The Beatles ("Anna"), The Rolling Stones ("You Better Move On"), and Bob Dylan ("Sally Sue Brown"), born in Sheffield, Alabama (d. 1993)[1][2]
Sally James [Cann], British children's TV-show presenter (Tiswas), born in Chiswick, London
Sly Dunbar, Jamaican reggae drummer (Sly & Robbie), born in Kingston, Jamaica

Bakithi Kumalo, South African session and touring bassist, and composer (Paul Simon - "Graceland"; "Call Me Al"; Mickey Hart's Planet Drum; Harry Belafonte), born in Alexandra, Johannesburg, South Africa
Yu Suzuki, Japanese game designer and creator of Virtua Fighter series, born in Kamaishi, Japan
First Barbary War: The Barbary pirates of Tripoli declare war on the United States of America (first US foreign war)
Italian navigatorAmerigo Vespuccileaves for his first voyage to the New World (disputed)
Napoleondefeats Austria in Battle of Lodi Bridge
1st steamboat to navigate the Mississippi River arrives at Fort Snelling
British House of Commons and Holborn Theatre in London damaged during German air raid
Stanley Cup Final, Chicago Stadium, Chicago, IL: Yvan Cournoyer has a goal and 2 assists as Montreal Canadiens beat Chicago Blackhawks, 6-4 to take title, 4 games to 2
Kate Gosselin gives birth to sextuplets in Hershey, Pennsylvania
Bill Waltonis inducted into the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame
"Twister" disaster film written byMichael CrichtonstarringHelen Huntand Bill Paxton premieres
Dr. T Berry Brazelton, American child development paediatrician, author, TV host (What Every Baby Knows), who developed the Neonatal Behavioral Assessment Scale (NBAS), born in Waco, Texas (d. 2018)
Larry Williams, American R&B singer-songwriter ("Dizzy, Miss Lizzie"; "Slow Down"), born in New Orleans, Louisiana (d. 1980)
Carl Douglas, Jamaican-British singer ("Kung Fu Fighting"), born in Kingston, Jamaica
Jay Ferguson, American rocker (Spirit - "I Got A Line on You"; solo - "Thunder Island") and composer (theme for "The Office"), born in Burbank, California
Kikki Danielsson, Swedish pop and country singer, yodeler, and accordion player, born in Osby, Sweden
Bacon's Rebellion begins, frontiersmen vs Virginia government
1st US Navy ship, the "United States," is launched
Centennial Fair opens in Philadelphia
